title: Video based microscope adjustment
abstract: The present invention relates to anoptical observation device which is controlled in a sterility preserving manner, and to a corresponding controlling program and/or program storage medium.
claims:
Claims Attorney's File: 61 914 P WO 2 XV Applicant: Brainlab AG Claims 1. An optical observation device (1) comprising: a) a main structure (2) comprising at least one optical camera (4); b) a motorized support structure (5) adapted to variably position the main structure (2) at or near a workplace to be observed; and c) a control unit (6) adapted to perform the following procedural steps: - receive a sequence of images from the at least one optical camera (4); - search a current image from the sequence of images for a trackable object (7) and track the trackable object (7) shown in the sequence of images subsequent to the current image; - receive a trigger signal for controlling the motorized support structure (5), the optical set-up (3) and/or the at least one optical camera (4) in accordance with a motion of the trackable object (7). 2. The optical observation device according to claim 1, wherein the main structure (2) is a microscope, particularly a surgical microscope. 3. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the support structure (5) is controlled to move the main structure (2) in accordance with the motion of the trackable object (7), wherein the support structure (5) is in particular an articulated arm with at least two arm sections (8) which are hingedly connected to each other via at least one joint (9), specifically via at least one motorized joint (9). 4. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the control unit (6) is adapted to receive the trigger signal via a sterility preserving user interface (10), particularly via covering or uncovering an optically recognizable feature (12) provided on the 

 trackable object (7), via a foot pedal switch (11), via voice command and/or via gestures which are recognizable from the sequence of images. 5. The optical observation device according to claim 4, wherein covering a designated feature (12) of the trackable object (7), which is visible to the at least one camera (4) is recognized as a gesture provoking the trigger signal. 6. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 3 to 5, wherein the motion of the main structure (2) is coupled to the motion of the trackable object (7) via at least one, particularly selectable conversion factor causing - the motion of the main structure (2) to be amplified as compared to the motion of the trackable object (7); - the motion of the main structure (2) to be reduced as compared to the motion of the trackable object (7); and/or - the motion of the main structure (2) to be damped as compared to the motion of the trackable object (7). 7. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein an upper threshold for the speed of motion of the trackable object (7) is defined and motions above the threshold are ignored. 8. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein at least two degrees of freedom (x, y, z, u, v, w) for the motion of the main structure (2) are controlled separately, such that motion of the main structure (2) in at least one of the at least two degrees of freedom (x, y, z, u, v, w) is blocked while the main structure (2) is controlled to move within at least one other of the at least two degrees of freedom (x, y, z, u, v, w). 9. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the control unit (6) is also adapted to control at least one of 

 - the properties of AR-content superimposed with the sequence of images, particularly the location, orientation and/or size of AR-content with respect to the sequence of images; - the optical set-up (3), particularly the focus-point of the optical set-up (3); and - the at least one optical camera (4), particularly the zoom-factor of the at least one optical camera (4); in accordance with a motion of the trackable object (7). 10. The optical observation device according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein a handheld medical instrument (7), particularly a pointer instrument (7) is recognized as trackable object (7), which is in particular provided as a disposable device (7). 11. The optical observation device according to claim 10, wherein the instrument (7) is tracked via a ring pattern (13) provided on the instrument (7) and visible in the sequence of images. 12. A program which, when running on a processor of the control unit (6) according to any one of the claims 1 to 11, causes the processor to perform the procedural steps, and/or a program storage medium on which the program is stored, in particular in a non-transitory form.
